title: eupatorium rugosum var. roanense
intro: Eupatorium rugosum var. roanense, also known as the white snake root or black snake root, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its hairy stems and clusters of small white flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its roots, which contain sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eupatorium rugosum var. roanense include its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential in modern times to inhibit the growth of certain cancer cells and bacteria. In herbal preparations, the root of the plant is typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
